zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de la Lee於1239年出生在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利時，他的父親Sir John De La Lee44歲，母親Rei M. Lega38歲。他於1251年與Petronilla Corbet在英國英格蘭史洛普爾皮爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1319年在他的家鄉享年80歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾波莫 希斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de la Lee于1239年出生在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利时，他的父亲Sir John De La Lee44岁，母亲Rei M. Lega38岁。他于1251年与Petronilla Corbet在英国英格兰史洛普爾皮爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1319年在他的家乡享年80岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾波莫 希斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de la Lee was born in 1239, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir John De La Lee, was 44 and his mother, Rei M. Lega, was 38. He married Petronilla Corbet in 1251, in Pimhill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1319, in his hometown, at the age of 80, and was buried in Bomere Heath, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas de la Lee est né en 1239, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John De La Lee, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Rei M. Lega, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Petronilla Corbet en 1251, à Pimhill, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1319, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Bomere Heath, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas de la Lee 1239 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John De La Lee 44 und seine Mutter Rei M. Lega 38. Er hat 1251 in Pimhill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Petronilla Corbet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1319 im Alter von 80 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Bomere Heath, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de la Lee è nato il nel 1239 nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John De La Lee, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Rei M. Lega, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Petronilla Corbet nel 1251, nel Pimhill, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1319, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Bomere Heath, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de la Leeが1239年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで生まれた時，父Sir John De La Leeは44歳，母Rei M. Legaは38歳であった。1251年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアピムヒルでPetronilla Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1319年に故郷で80歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアボメア ヒースにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de la Lee(이)가 1239년 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John De La Lee(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Rei M. Lega(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1251년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 피힐에서 Petronilla Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1319년 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 봄르 히스에 묻혔다.

mn.Pevenhull, Pimhill, Shropshire, England,: Pimhill, Salopesberia, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas de la Lee nasceu em 1239, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John De La Lee, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Rei M. Lega, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Petronilla Corbet em 1251, em Pimhill, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1319, em sua cidade natal, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Bomere Heath, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de la Lee родился в 1239 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John De La Lee было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Rei M. Lega, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronilla Corbet в 1251 г. в местности пимхилл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1319 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности бомир хит, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas de la Lee nació en 1239, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John De La Lee, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Rei M. Lega, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Petronilla Corbet en 1251, en Pimhill, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1319, en su pueblo, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en Bomere Heath, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
